# Getting support

Getting help shouldn't feel like pulling teeth, so I've made it as easy as possible to connect with me and find the answers you need.

Whether you're using one of my projects, contributing to my repos, or just exploring my code, I'll do my best to point you in the right direction.

No question is too small, no problem too weird — I've probably seen it all (and if I haven't, that makes it more interesting)!

## 🎯 Where to go for what

### 💬 GitHub Discussions — the community hangout

**Perfect for:** Open-ended conversations, brainstorming, showing off what you built, getting design feedback, or just saying hi.

[Join the conversation →](https://github.com/whallin/.github/discussions)

**What works well here:**

- "How do you handle X in your projects?"
- "I built this cool thing using your code — thoughts?"
- "What's the best approach for..."
- "Anyone else run into this pattern?"
- General architecture and design questions.

### 🐛 GitHub Issues — when you need specific help

**Perfect for:** Specific problems, clear questions, bug reports, feature requests, or anything that needs tracking and follow-up.

- **🐞 Bug Report** — Something's broken and you want it fixed.
- **✨ Feature Request** — You've got ideas for making things better.
- **❓ Question & Support** — You need help with usage, setup, or understanding how something works.
- **📚 Documentation** — The docs could be clearer (we love these!)
- **⚡ Performance** — Something's slower than a sleepy sloth.

[Browse all templates →](https://github.com/whallin/.github/issues/new/choose)

### 📧 Direct contact — for personal support

Sometimes you need to talk to me directly, and that's totally fine!

- **General questions:** [william@hallin.media](mailto:william@hallin.media)
- **Website:** [https://hallin.media/](https://hallin.media/)
- **Security issues:** See our [Security Policy](SECURITY.md)

> [!IMPORTANT]
> I'm just one person, so I might take a day or two to respond — but I will get back to you!

## 📖 Help yourself (the best kind of selfishness)

Before reaching out, these resources might already have what you're looking for:

### Diving into documentation

- **Repository README files** — Each project has setup instructions, usage examples, and common gotchas.
- **Wiki pages** — For the deep-dive technical docs and architectural decisions.
- **API documentation** — If I have it, it's probably linked from the README.
- **Code examples** — Look for `examples/` or `demos/` folders in repositories.

### Search like a detective

- **GitHub search** — Search issues and discussions across all my repositories. Someone might've asked your question!
- **Previous issues** — Closed issues are goldmines of troubleshooting wisdom.
- **Pull request descriptions** — Sometimes, the best explanations are in PR descriptions and review comments.

> [!TIP]
> Search for error messages in quotes — like "TypeError: Cannot read property" — to find exact matches.

## 🤝 How to get better help faster

### Making questions easy to answer

**Give me context:**

- What are you trying to achieve? (the bigger picture helps!)
- What did you try already?
- What happened vs. what you expected?
- Your environment (OS, versions, browser, etc.)

**Share relevant details:**

- Error messages (the full thing, not just "it doesn't work")
- Code snippets (but please format them nicely!)
- Screenshots or recordings (a picture is worth a thousand words)

**Keep it focused:**

- One question per issue/discussion.
- Specific titles ("Login form validation" vs. "Form problems")
- Clear, concise descriptions.

### What makes me happy

**Good examples:**

- "The documentation says to do A, but I'm getting B instead. Am I missing something?"
- "I found what looks like a bug in the email validation. Here's how to reproduce it..."
- "I'm trying to implement user authentication using X, but when I do Y, I get error Z. Here's my code..."

**Not so helpful:**

- "It doesn't work"
- "Your software is broken"
- "This is urgent!!!" (without explaining why)

## 🌟 Community support

### Help others, help yourself

The best way to get great support? Give great support! When you answer questions, even if you're not an expert, sharing your experience helps others who might be facing similar challenges.

If you find documentation that's confusing or unclear, don't struggle through it — fix it or let me know how to improve it. When you solve a tricky problem, take a moment to share how you did it, because your solution might save the next person hours of debugging.

And remember when you were new to all of this? Pay it forward by welcoming newcomers and helping them find their footing.

### Become a community hero

Regular contributors often become unofficial community helpers, and that's awesome! If you're interested in taking on a more formal role in helping with my projects, reach out to [william@hallin.media](mailto:william@hallin.media).

---

_If you've read this far and still can't find what you need, email me at [william@hallin.media](mailto:william@hallin.media) and I'll help you._
